blockingIssues.description: `quantdb/extract_v2.py` does not implement the required `NCBITaxon_9606 -> human` species alias, so `_translate_species()` fails one documented input variant.

`extract_fasc_fib_v2()` only scans cached `path_metadata` for CSV entries even though the real f006 `path-metadata.json` contains zero CSV entries, so the function returns no fascicles or fibers for the real dataset.

The new integration tests skip when `_find_csv_entries(path_metadata)` is empty, which is the real f006 condition, so the asserted real-data fascicle/fiber counts never execute and the tests do not prove the feature contract.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `.factory/library/extraction-layer-validation.md` documenting the required `NCBITaxon_9606`/`ncbitaxon:9606` species aliases for extraction-layer entity parsing.

Added `.factory/library/extraction-layer-validation.md` documenting that the cached f006 `path-metadata.json` contains zero CSV entries, so real CSV extraction needs discovery beyond cached metadata scanning.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update formatter-fix guidance in `db-pipeline-worker` (or related mission guidance) to prefer the repository's existing pre-commit Blue hook over installing formatter packages into the shared conda environment.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-extraction-formatting` review found worker session `9cc8a185-eacd-4a16-a580-f70a3cca08dc` installed `blue` with pip even though AGENTS forbids new package installs without approval and `.pre-commit-config.yaml` already defines the Blue hook used to validate the files.
